Introduction to the Simplex
⚠ Figure failed to load

plan.contextChunk:
classes, is present in the image. This is typical in settings where most
of the images contain a single large object. Multiclass classification
with exclusive classes results is given in the following constraint:

$$\sum_{c=1}^K \hat{y}_c=1 
$${#eq-object_recognition-sum_to_one_constraint}

where $0>\hat{y}_c>1$. In the toy example with three classes shown
previously, the valid solutions for
$\hat{\mathbf{y}}=[\hat{y}_1, \hat{y}_2, \hat{y}_3]^\mathsf{T}$ are
constrained to lie within a **simplex**.

:::{.column-margin}
Simplex with three exclusive classes:

![](figures/object_recognition/simplex_v2.png){width="100%"}
:::


Under this formulation, the function $f$ is a mapping
$f:\mathbb{R}^{N \times M \times 3} \rightarrow \vartriangle^{K-1}$ from
the set of red-green-blue (RGB) images to the $(K-1)$-dimensional
simplex, $\vartriangle^{K-1}$.

The function $f$ is constrained to belong to a family of possible
functions. For instance, $f$ might belong to the space of all the
functions that can be built with a neural network. In such a case the
function is specified by the network parameters $\theta: f_\theta$. When
using a neural net, the vector $\hat{\mathbf{y}}$ is usually computed as
the output of a softmax layer.

interactions.label: Axis Scale
interactions.teaches: Adjusts the scale of the axes to see how the simplex adapts to different scales.

interactions.label: Toggle View
interactions.teaches: Switches between a normal view and a top view to better understand the simplex orientation.

demo_steps.title: Introduction to the Simplex
demo_steps.narration: Here we see a 3D simplex, representing possible solutions for three exclusive classes: Bike, Car, and Bird. Each corner represents full confidence in one class.

demo_steps.title: Scaling the Axes
demo_steps.narration: As we increase the scale of the axes, notice how the simplex maintains its shape. This shows the relative nature of class probabilities.

demo_steps.title: Top View Analysis
demo_steps.narration: Switch to the top view to see how the simplex is projected on the 2D plane. This view highlights how probabilities sum to one along the simplex.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Start with a 3D perspective that shows the simplex plane clearly and allow rotation.
interactionPlan.notes: Ensure the labels remain aligned with their respective axes as the view toggles.
